at 6:30 p.m. on october 7, 2019, an employee had just parked his tractor (caterpillar challenger tractor) with a scraper (noble bowl scraper) attachment for the evening. a coworker operating another tractor with the same attachment pulled up behind the employee to park his tractor. the employee was giving the coworker hand signals to help him park right behind the employee's tractor. whenthe coworker stopped his tractor and released the clutch, the employee walked between the two tractors at the same time. when the coworker released the clutch, the tractor moved forward and pinned the employee between the two tractors. the employee was taken to the hospital, where he later died of unspecified injuries.

at 9:30 p.m. on october 10, 2019, an employee was performing preventative maintenance on the equipment when his finger came in contact with a rotating chain. the employee suffered an amputation of a fingertip and was treated without hospitalization.

at 12:15 p.m. on september 26, 201, an employee was using a nail gun to hammer nails into a concrete wall when he pushed the nail gun against the wall, the scaffolding he was standing on tilted and he lost his balance. the employee tried to regain his balance but fell forward between the wall and the scaffolding to the concrete floor below. the employee was hospitalized to treatinjuries to the vertebrae in his neck and back.

at 4:00 p.m. on october 2, 2019, an employee was working as a farm worker at a fruit and tree nut farm. although there was a language barrier, he was not a migrant farm worker. he was operating an almond sweeper. the sweeper's main control lever had disengaged from the transmission. as a result, the employee lost operational control of whether the sweeper would be moving in forward or reverse. the employee panicked because the machine was traveling downhill rapidly. he made a judgement call to stop the sweeper by steering it into a treeand jumping out. this piece of equipment did not have rops or a seat belt. as the employee was jumping from the machine, the sweeper hit the tree. the employee's upper body struck the steering wheel before the employee hit the ground. the employee suffered the fracture of multiple ribs on his right side, and one rib punctured a lung. the employee was hospitalized.

at 1:30 a.m. on october 22, 2019, an employee working for a plastics products manufacturer was operating a fork truck. the employee caught his right hand fingers between a vertical metal aspirator stand and the side of the forklift rail. the employee amputated his pinky finger and required medical treatment but not hospitalization.

at 1:40 p.m. on november 5, 2019, an employee who worked maintenance for a plastic bottles manufacturer was assisting a coworker adjusting a flap on the bottom of a conveyor belt. a maintenance crew had replaced a conveyor unit and part of the work required the employee to change out the new conveyor's plug connection to match the receptacle.  in the course of making an adjustment to the conveyor flap, the employee placed his left hand on a metal chute located on the associated injection molding machine and his right hand on the conveyor frame. at this time, he sustained an electrical shock from the 270 volt equipment. the ground wire in the conveyor's plug connection was incorrectly wired as a phase instead of connected to the ground screw. there was one witness to the incident. electrical shock from 270v equipment. improper wiring of the conveyor plug connection.  the employee was hospitalized for treatment.

at 4:45 p.m. on may 18, 2019, an employee was installing a metal i-beam for an automatic feed system when the safety latch on the hook failed to secure the strap. the employee incurred skull and arm fractures and was hospitalized.

at 4:30 p.m. on november 14, 2019, employee #1 was trimming a tree to clear a right-of-way. as the employee turned to reconnect the "stick saw", the piece of wood released and struck the employee, killing him.

at 6:15 a.m. on november 13, 2019, employee #1 and employee #2 were checking filter baskets to clear debris on the powder fines scrubber strainer.  employee # 1 checked a strainer that did not have a flow and a basket while employee #2 observed.  employee #1 returned the basket to the strainer and heard a pop thatwas followed by the release of hot water.  employee #1 sustained 2nd and 3rd degree burns to his arm and chest and was hospitalized. employee #2 sustained 2nd degree burns to his arms and waist and was treated without hospitalization.

at 2:00 p.m. on november 18, 2019, employee #1 was washing out the hydro vacuumtruck debris body/tank with a high pressure water hose. a coworker said that he saw employee #1 give the hand signal in the passenger side mirror to close the tailgate door of the debris body/tank.  the coworker got out of the truck and operated the levers to close and lock the tailgate door then got back into the truck to wait on employee #1 to return to the truck cab.  when employee #1 didn't come to the truck cab after approximately 3 to 5 minutes, the coworker went to look for him. the coworker found employee #1 caught between the tailgate door and debris body/tank. employee #1 had sustained blunt force trauma across his shoulders and chest and was killed.

at 6:02 p.m. on november 20, 2019, employee #1 was conducting maintenance/repairoperations (e.g.,  connecting weights to a clamp of an excavator boom).  the excavator boom arm then struck employee #1, who then sustained internal injuries and was killed.

at 10:30 a.m. on november 21, 2019, an employee working as a manager for a toolmanufacturer was setting up a new jointer.  the employee was in the process of adjusting the knives while running a test board through the jointer. the board kicked backed and caused the employee to strike the knives with his hand. the employee partially amputated his middle finger, requiring medical treatment without hospitalization.

at 10:45 a.m. on november 15, 2019, an employee was operating a milwaukee battery powered circular saw (serial number: f31ad1837) above his head to cut a wall stud. the sawblade was pinched by the stud being cut, causing the saw to kick backwards, and the employee's right finger was amputated by the rotating sawblade. the employee was treated without hospitalization.

at 7:00 p.m. on november 25, 2019, an employee was loading a titanium rod (stock) held in the stock feeder (rhino bar) as a part of a set up operation to produce dental implant bases.  the stock was loaded into the cnc chuck (holding device for the stock) that feeds the stock (on the z axis) and moves the stock back and forth during the production phase.  the stock feed chuck is on the right hand side of the machine and is separated from the point of operation for the milling by a dividing wall in the machine.  the stock is fed through an opening in this dividing wall and the milling takes place on the left side of the machine.   during the set up and loading operation, the operator uses a switch below the rhino bar, below and to the right of the control panel to switch from automatic to manual mode.  then, the stock feeder is loaded and returned to the feed position.  the rod stock is then fed into the chuck and through the dividing wall and set into position by pressing the chuck close button on the control panel.  the z axis switch is then toggled to align stock to tooling.  it was when the employee toggled the z axis control switch that the machine acted as if it was in automatic mode and the chuck began to go into workmode and moved to the left while the employee's hand was in the line of fire. this crushed the employee's thumb and index finger on his left hand, causing the amputation and partial amputation.  the employee had to manipulate the z axis switch to remove the chuck from his hand, to free it from the machine.  the employee confirmed the auto/manual switch was in the manual mode. the employee was transported to, treated, and hospitalized at uci irvine.  the employer is a dental device manufacturer.  the employee is a machine operator, who operates cnc machines (decco 10 cnc machine #15) in the production of dental devices.

at 4:30 p.m. on november 22, 2019, an employee was using a jogging machine to fix blue film on metal product. the employee pressed the wrong button and the shear cycled and took off the tips of his left hand index and middle fingers. the employee was treated without hospitalization.

at 9:00 a.m. on november 26, 2019, an employee used a step ladder to climb on top of a mold in order to work while squatting on top of the mold. a coworker was on the step ladder assisting him and handing him tools as needed. the employee was startled and fell from the mold to the concrete floor approximately seven feet. the employee was hospitalized to treat head and back injuries.

at 7:45 p.m. on november 26, 2019, an employee was on the barn roof while repairing a loose portion of the steel roof panel.  the employee fell approximately 20 feet to the barn's dirt floor when the roof collapsed and was killed.

at approximately 02:28 p.m. on june 25, 2019, the employee, serving as a carpenter's assistant, was assigned to cut a piece of wood that was 8 foot long by 2 inches wide to have a width of 1? inches wide on a 10 inch delta unisaw tilting arbor table saw. the employee's usual duties do not normally include making these kinds of small cuts on the table saw. the employee had raised the guard above the saw blade and made the cut; however, the saw did not have a spreader. the employee stated that while making the cut, the saw blade started vibrating and threw the wood back at them, and the fingers were cut by the sawblade. the employee was admitted and treated at the hospital for injuries on the left hand including partial amputations on the ring finger to the second knuckle, pinky finger to the bottom of the nailbed, disfigurement of the index finger, and fractures on the thumb and middle finger. at 08:57 p.m. on june 26, 2019, cosh was notified of the incident from the orange county fire authority as the employer did not comply with reporting requirements.

at 4:40 a.m. on december 2, 2019, an employee was operating an electric pallet jack in a congested area. the employee tripped and fell backwards, and the pallet jack ran up over and onto his right foot as he fell. the employee was hospitalized to treat a fractured right foot.

at 6:30 a.m. on november 23, 2019, an employee was standing next to a forklift, speaking with the forklift operator. a person operating a hyster forklift (model 110ft, serial number u005v05054r) backed up, pinning the employee's left leg between the two forklifts. the employee sustained several fractures to the left leg and was hospitalized.

at 10:45 p.m. on december 21, 2019, an employee was checking the operation of a washer. the washer behind him had a pressurization event, blowing hot water/steam onto the employee's back.  the employee was hospitalized to treat the burns.

at 7:00 p.m. on december 13, 2019, an employee was removing finished product from the exit portion of the trim press. the employee observed excess material caught in the output portion of the trim press. the employee reached under the barrier guarding and over the packing table to pull the excess out, while the equipment was in operation. the employee was struck between the machine and the right trim die. the employee sustained an amputation of a fingertip.  the employee was treated without hospitalization.

at 8:20 a.m. on january 8, 2020, employee #1 and a coworker were moving equipment down an alley as part of a residential renovation project.  employee #1 was operating a ditch witch trencher and his coworker was operating an aerial lift behind employee #1.  the aerial lift rear-ended the trencher, and employee #1 sustained internal crushing injuries and was killed.

at 11:30 p.m. on january 7, 2020, an employee was descending a ladder into a trench when he lost his balance and reached out and his hand went into the drive chain for the trench conveyor. the trench conveyor was running at the time the employee was entering the trench. the employee suffered amputations of part of his right index finger and his right middle finger and was treated without hospitalization.

at 2:30 p.m. on january 10, 2020, an employee was rolling up tow straps and was caught between a serco ab8700 airbag dock leveler and a truck.  the employee was struck in the head when a load fell off of a forklift's tines and fractured his skull, killing him.

at 7:45am on january 14, 2020, an employee was getting ready to install a gas tank. the employee was moving a jack used to lift the gas tank in place.  the employee accidently activated the jack, causing the index and middle finger to be pinched between the jack and the bottom of the vehicle.  the employee suffered smashed finger tips and was hospitalized.

at 1:45 p.m. on january 22, 2020, an employee was removing a part from a forming machine when his glove became caught between the ejector plate and air line. two coworkers  came to the employee's aid and helped him release his finger. the employee amputated his fingertip, requiring hospitalization.

at 9:00 a.m. on january 27, 2020, an employee stood on a manhole ladder to receive a board lowered down to them. while attempting to grab the wood, the employee lost balance and fell approximately fifteen feet. the employee was hospitalized to treat a facial fracture and various contusions to the body.

at 4:30 a.m. on january 9, 2020, an employee and coworker #1 were performing maintenance work drilling out and replacing bolts for an access panel on the pusher table of an auto depalletizing robot (robot #2) within a robot cell. at the same time, coworker #2 was preparing to test the operational functions of the dunnage robot (robot #1). coworker #2 completed maintenance work on robot #1 while the employee and coworker #1 remained inside the robot cell. coworker #2 exited the robot cell to reset the light curtains. the employee and coworker #1 had entered the robot cell through the light curtains and that neither had isolated the electrical and pneumatic energy sources at the controls on robot #2 or affixed lockout devices before performing maintenance work. coworker #2 resetthe robot cell perimeter light curtains for robot #1 and robot # 2 in preparation for a test run on robot #1. the robot #2 pusher table and robot armturned on after the perimeter light curtains were reset and the run cycle was initiated in automatic mode on the operator control panel. the employee and coworker #1 were both sitting on the robot #2 pusher table while repairing damaged bolts on the table, so the pusher arm sensor for the robot #2 pusher armdetected something on the table that needed to be pushed off. coworker #1 managed to get off the pusher table without injury, but the employee was unable to move in time. the employee's feet had dropped down into the recessed area where they had removed an access plate from the table. his feet were repeatedly struck by the pusher arm as the robot tried to complete the normal programmed action of clearing materials from the table outgoing conveyors. his coworkers hit the emergency stop buttons on the outside of the robot cell and helped the employee get off the table and out of the robot cell. the employee was transported to a hospital and admitted to treat a laceration on one foot and extensive bruising and compartment syndrome on both feet.

at 12:00 p.m. on january 29, 2020, an employee used a circular saw to cut wood. at some point, the employee's right thumb contacted the saw blade and amputated. the employee was taken to the hospital and admitted for treatment.

at 2:00 p.m. on june 17, 2019, an employee was moving and setting up staging equipment when his finger was pinched between a truss and a foot pad. the employee suffered a fingertip laceration and was treated without hospitalization.

at 12:00 a.m. on february 15, 2020, an employee was working from an aerial lift basket to remove bridge column formwork. at some point, the employee was having trouble removing a section of plywood form and stepped out of the aerial lift basket without connecting his life-line to an anchor point. as the employee stepped onto the plywood platform, the surface shifted and the employee fell approximately thirty feet. the employee was hospitalized with a punctured lung, fractures to the left arm and leg, but later died of his injuries. injuries.

at 8:45 a.m. on february 13, 2020, an employee, a plant manager, and 3 coworkers were trying to right a soda machine which had been knocked over by vandals. two of the four workers were using j-bars to lift the machine; the employee and one coworker were not. the coworker without a j-bar let go of his part of the soda machine which caused it to fall and crush the employee's left little finger. the other two coworker assisting with j-bars were not injured. the employee suffered a left little fingertip amputation and was treated without hospitalization.

at 9:25 a.m. on february 12, 2020, an employee was ensuring a felt pipe liner was saturated with resin while straddling a moving conveyor when his foot and leg slipped and were caught in the roller. the employee was hospitalized for treatment, and his leg was surgically amputated below the knee.

at 2:00 p.m. on march 11, 2019, employee #1 was using a scissor lift to access a gas station canopy.  the lift was elevated above canopy when it came into contact with overhead electrical power lines.  the employee was shocked and burned.  the employee was transported to the hospital and admitted for treatment of second and third degree burns.

at 8:30 a.m. on march 2, 2020, an employee working for a tree care service was hooking a nylon winch rope up to a tree stump to be pulled into a chipper. the rope tightened around his fingers. the employee was hospitalized and required surgical amputation of his index, middle and ring fingers.

at approximately 9:30 a.m. on february 21, 2020, employee #1, working for a contracting company, was tasked with installing new roofing dry in material on a new build residential home.  as the employee stepped onto the roof from the ladder the employee's foot slipped, the employee lost balance, and then fell approximately 12 feet to the ground below. employee #1 was hospitalized and treated for back fracture.

at 4:00 p.m. on february 11, 2020, an employee working as a machine operator for a cement manufacturer was reaching into a cement mixer to clean it. the mixer machine was off but the blade was still moving. the employee caught his hand on the mixer blade. he sustained lacerations to his right arm and was hospitalized.

at 12:19 p.m. on october 29, 2019, an employee working for a logistics warehouse was operating a raymond model-8410 double pallet jack in an aisle. the employeestated that he did not remember what happened when he found himself on the ground. coworkers reported that the employee lost control of the piv and hit the rack. one coworker reported seeing the employee fall to the ground. the employeestated that he was not trained and evaluated on the double pallet jack and toldhis supervisor that he was not feeling comfortable operating the piv. he had been trained on the single pallet jack. the employee sustained a concussion and fractured his pelvis and right leg and required hospitalization for approximately two weeks. while in the hospital, he required surgery on his pelvic area and right leg.
an employee was working at a boarding school run by a federal bureau. she was exposed to covid-19. at 6:00 a.m. on march 27, 2020, she died. this was an osha-covered fatality.

at 9:45 a.m. on november 11, 2019, an employee and a coworker were replacing fence posts damaged in a recent wildfire in an open field on a ranch. the employee was holding metal fence strands away from the auger on the post hole digger while the coworker was operating the john deere 850 tractor and the m500 post hole digger implement attached to and powered by the tractor. the post-hole digger was operated by the controls on the tractor that engaged the power take-off shaft and the hydraulics for the three point-hitch. while the employeewas holding the fence strands with the digging pole, the coworker was moving the tractor to position the auger.  the coworker had the pto shaft engaged which powered the rotating auger while trying to position it when the employee reached out with his right arm and contacted the auger and became entangled. the employee was hospitalized to treat multiple fractures and soft tissue injuries to his right arm.

at 6:00 a.m. on october 31, 2019, employee #1 and employee #2 were unclogging cotton from a condenser and lint chute.  when the condenser's panel cover was open, smoldering cotton was introduced to oxygen, resulting in a flash fire. employee #1 was hospitalized for treatment of burns to the face, hands, and arms.   employee #2 sustained burns to the arm and face and was treated withouthospitalization.

on march 31, 2020, employee # 1 called out sick from work via voicemail. he worked as nursing supervisor within the facility. employee # 1 died on thursday, april 2, 2020 possibly of covid-19. it is possible that he was exposed to the virus within the facility in the course of his work. on april 2, 2020, the facility was notified of employee # 1's death.

at 6:15 p.m. on may 6, 2020, an employee was loading a truck with corn grain, it is unknown why the employee climbed a ladder and entered a 42 foot diameter grain ben.  he was engulfed in the grain.  he was killed from asphyxiation due to inhalation of corn grain engulfment.

at 10:30 p.m. on april 8, 2020, an employee was working for a wholesaler of farm supplies. he was working as a sales representative. he was working at the facility's anhydrous ammonia load-out. he had just finished filling a nurse tank with anhydrous ammonia. because he was not familiar with the connection's new valve, he did not realize how much pressure remained in the line. when he disconnected the valve, gas and liquid ammonia struck him in the face. he inhaled some of the gas, and his lungs and respiratory system suffered injuries. he was hospitalized for two or more days.

at 9:30 a.m. on april 29, 2020, an employee was working by herself to trim a peach tree within the orchard. according to the employee, she erected a stokes orchard ladder, climbed up it and was working from the eighth rung from the bottom. the back tripod leg was not stable and slipped out from the climbing face, causing the employee to fall 10 feet to the ground. the foreman called for emergency medical services, and the employee was taken to adventist rideout memorial hospital in marysville, ca. the employee was released that same day. the employee sustained a laceration to her left leg below her kneecap, which required 3 stitches to close. she also sustained torn or damaged left knee cartilage, as well as a contusion on her back.

at 12:00 midnight on february 4, 2020, an employee was serving drinks at the barwhen she poured a drink for a customer. the customer tasted the drink and complained about the taste, so the employee also tasted the drink. the employeeimmediately felt a burning sensation to her lips and mouth. a beer line cleaning solution had been put into a liquor bottle and was not marked with its contents. the employee suffered a light burning sensation to her lips and mouth, but no tissue damage occurred.  the employee was treated without hospitalization.

an employee was working for a firm that manufactured cat and dog food. he was working at a warehouse. he operated a forklift and performed other tasks at the workplace. on or about april 23, 2020, he felt ill and did not report to work. at noon on april 26, 2020, he was hospitalized. on that same day, his employer was notified that the employee had tested positive for covid-19 and had been hospitalized. contact tracing led the employer to believe that the exposure had occurred at work.
at approximately 12:00 p.m. on may 1, 2020, an employee was treating patients as a doctor and became  infected with covid-19.  the employee later died from complications from the virus.

at 12:00 a.m. on may 26, 2020, an employee and a coworker were working to install an approximate four foot by twenty foot debris platform on a bridge. the platform was attached to cat excavator by two chains on each end of the platform. once the platform was in place the employee and coworker stepped ontothe platform to remove the chains. the coworker finished removing the chains and stepped off of the platform while the employee worked to remove the last chain. the excavator operator began to raise the lift chains causing the platform to rise on one side. the employee lost his balance and fell thirty-three feet and into the water. the employee was killed by drowning.

at 2:30 p.m. on may 19, 2020, an employee was working for a nationwide chain of big box stores that sold electrical supplies, plumbing supplies, appliances, lumber, and so forth. she was working at one of the firm's stores as a customer service representative. she and a coworker, also a customer service representative, were replenishing the store's inventory of peat moss. the coworker was operating a sit-down forklift. the coworker was using the forklift to retrieve a full pallet of peat moss from the top rack of some storage shelves. the pallet was approximately 16 feet (4.9 meters) up. the employee was sitting outside the barricaded zone of a warehouse aisle performing spotting duties for her coworker. the pile of the bags of peat moss was not stable. as the coworker removed the full pallet of peat moss, nearly half the bags fell off the pallet to the ground. the employee crossed over into the barricaded zone to help pick up the bags from off the ground. there were still some bags elevated 16 feet (4.9 meters) up. two more bags of peat moss fell off the pallet, striking the employee in her shoulder and leg. she suffered fractures in her right ankle and foot. she was hospitalized. the employer reported the accident to the division dosh learned about the accident at approximately 4:44 a.m. on may 20, 2020. the employer reported the accident within the required timeframe. the division arrived at employer's site at approximately 11:00 a.m. on june 5, 2020, to initiate an investigation. a contributing factor to the incident was the employee's entering the barricaded area of the working aisle. this was contrary to the training provided by the employer to the employee and the coworker. no accident-related citations were to be issued.

an employee was working at a general medical and surgical hospital. she worked in a primary care facility, where she provided direct care to patients, including those with positive covid-19 infections. she developed symptoms consistent with a covid-19 infection. the covid-19 viral infection was confirmed. at 4:00 p.m. on may 14, 2020, she died. this was an osha-covered fatality.

at 3:00 p.m. on april 20, 2020, an employee, a maintenance department electrician/mechanic, was working in the company's storage/wood shop with a coworker, a manager from another field office. the coworker had requested the employee's assistance in cutting a piece of wood to fit in a panel hole. the employee was using an electric table saw, and a piece of wood he was cutting kicked back.  the employee stated that he used a delta 10-inch tilting arbor unisaw, model number  34-801f, serial number 87f31687, to cut a piece of hard wood, approx. 8 inches by 4 inches by 2 inches thick in size, with the coworker standing nearby watching. after he cut the wood in half, he attempted to cut the pieces into smaller pieces. the employee used a push stick to push the wood, but after the second piece was cut, the employee stated that the left cut piece unexpectedly flew back and struck the index finger on his right hand, causing the loss of the fingertip. the employee was driven to concentra occupational medical center in sacramento, where he was released the same day.  the employeealso sustained nerve damage and bone that needed to be grinded, with the skin peeled over the bone.

an employee was working for a company that made non-chocolate candy and confections. while in the course of performing his normal duties, he was exposed to covid-19.  at 9:00 a.m. on april 24, 2020, he was hospitalized. there were at least five other covid-19-related hospitalizations at this workplace. see investigation number 126804 and inspection number 1478004; investigation number 126810 and inspection number 1478053; investigation number 126812 and inspection number 1478058; investigation number 126813 and inspection number 1478059; and investigation number 126814 and inspection number 1478062. number 1478062.

at 12:45 p.m. on june 11, 2020, employee #1, employed by a construction company, was working from a height and cutting structural steel as part of the demolition of a coal conveyor.  the structure collapsed, and the employee fell.  the employee sustained severe blunt force trauma to his torso and was killed. killed.

at 10:45 a.m. on june 19, 2020, an employee was positioning an engineering rod equipped with a gps receiver on the end, at the end of a fence. the employee extended the rod approximately 24 feet through the canopy of a tree. the employee was shocked and killed when the engineering rod either contacted the power line or the power line arced.

at 8:30 a.m. on june 26, 2020, employee #1, employed by a roofing company, was on the roof of a three-story commercial building and preparing to begin roofing renovation work.  the employee fell, a fall height of 25 feet.  employee #1 was killed.
at 10:00 p.m. on may 31, 2020, an employee was adjusting a pipe, when the pipe came loose and struck the employee in the head. the employee was hospitalized to treat a bruised head.

at 5:45 a.m. on july 5, 2020, an employee was threading a wrapper into a doboy machine to process individually wrapped slices of bread when the wrap stuck at the first roll. the employee turned on the machine and attempted to slide the wrap by hand catching his left ring finger in the heated knife that cuts the wrapper between each individual package. the employee amputated the tip of left ring finger, requiring hospitalization.

at 10:30 p.m. on july 8, 2020, employee #1 and three coworkers, employed by a construction company, were doing excavation work in a landfill excavation shaft at a depth of approximately 35 to 40 feet.  two of the coworkers were operating excavators, while employee #1 and the third coworker were installing liner plate.  the operator of the excavator that had a bucket attached inadvertently hit the excavator's swing control.  the bucket, which was approximately 5 feet from the ground, swung and struck employee #1.  employee #1's skull was fractured, and he was killed.

at 1:45 p.m. on july 10, 2020, an employee working for a mechanical contractor was installing new ductwork in a commercial building when he fell from a ladder to the concrete floor below. the employee was hospitalized to treat broken bones in both feet and a fractured pelvis and a vertebra of the spine.

at 9:15 a.m. on january 8, 2020, an employee was preparing a mulch truck for a job when he became entangled in an auger in the back of the bt-90 trailer. the employee amputated his right arm as a result requiring hospitalization.

at 12:19 p.m. on august 9, 2020, an employee worked as a surgical nurse at the hospital and tested positive for the covid-19 infection. on july 10, 2020, the employee was admitted to baycare hospital in winter haven, fl.  on august 8, 2020, the employee died at st. joseph's hospital in tampa, fl due to cerebral hemorrhage from covid-19 infection.

an employee was providing private health care to a fragile child at a client house during the night shift when he was exposed to covid-19 by the parent of the child who did not show any covid-19 symptoms on the last day of service which was july 24, 2020. both the family and the employee took a covid-19 test on the same day. the employee's test result came back positive on july 27, 2020, while the family's test result came back positive on july 30, 2020. the employeewas admitted to a local hospital on august 3, 2020, because his pneumonia symptoms had become severe. the employee died on august 13, 2020.

an employee was working as a medical professional at a nursing care or skilled nursing facility. he worked with other medical professionals. on may 10, 2020, he died from covid-19-related issues, believed to be associated with treating patients who tested positive.

at 9:00 p.m. on may 8, 2020, an employee who had worked as a licensed practical nurse for a healthcare center died from covid-19. the employee returned from 49 days off work due to a broken foot and worked for 14 days before calling out sick. after calling out sick, the employee was admitted to the hospital and placed into a coma. the employer did not have an adequate respiratory protection program in place.

an employee was working at a general medical or surgical hospital. he was going about his normal work duties while in the facility. for some unknown reason, he became exposed to the covid-19 virus.  at 2:00 p.m. on july 16, 2020, he was hospitalized due to complications of covid-19.

at 4:30 p.m. on august 26, 2020, an employee lost control of a forklift, while training to operate a stand-up forklift.  the employee's leg became caught between the machine and a guardrail, causing the employee to sustain a broken leg. the employee was hospitalized.

on june 18, 2020, employee # 1, a police officer, worked his last day. he tested positive for covid-19 on june 24, 2020. he was hospitalized on august 3, 2020. the division learned of the illness from the employer on august 4, 2020 at approximately 3:00 p.m. the exposure may have occurred while employee # 1 was at a restrictive post at a kiosk located on campus. employee # 1 was admitted to the hospital with acute respiratory illness associated with covid-19 virus infection.

at 10:50 a.m. on september 3, 2020, an employee was putting on freezer clothing while driving a forklift. the employee encountered a slippery floor and lost control of the forklift. the employee was hospitalized to treat a broken left hand and amputation of the left thumb tip.

at approximately 12:55p.m. on or about august 25, 2020, employee #1 was assigned the task of painting the ceilings inside the steel drums room at a school. he was working with his supervisor. he fell from an elevated surface.  employee #1 was found unresponsive, lying on his back, with his head towards a 1-tier scaffold, commonly known as a bakers scaffold, and his feet towards the wall. employee #1 was killed.

at 9:33 a.m. on september 9, 2020, employee # 1 was processing red meat with a meat grinding machine. he was cleaning the machine as part of the process. the machine was stuck. employee # 1 unloaded the machine without turning it off or unplugging it. when employee # 1 removed the obstruction from the machine, the machine was activated and caught his right hand and ground it until his elbow. employee # 1 was hospitalized. he suffered an amputation of his right arm.

at 7:15 a.m. on august 31, 2020, an employee was working for a firm that owned several shortline railroads and provided maintenance services for rolling stock. he was a laborer, and he was working at one of the firm's railcar repair facilities. he was operating a case model 586g forklift, with serial number jjgn580gtac533346. he had stopped the forklift in front of the plate room building. he was getting off the forklift. the forklift began to move forward, and the employee's legs were run over by the forklift's left rear tire. the employee suffered a fractured right leg femur and multiple fractures to his lower left leg. he was hospitalized.

at 9:00 a.m. on july 27, 2020, an employee was working alone on a 125 psi pressurized system when a component broke and hit him in the face. the employeeincurred a gash to his forehead and broken right eye socket, requiring hospitalization.

at 4:15 p.m. on september 1, 2020, an employee was lubricating a shaft bearing, when the employee's finger got caught in the belt/pulley pinch point.  the employee amputated a fingertip and was treated without hospitalization.

at 11:45 a.m. on september 12, 2020, an employee working as a chainsaw operatorfor a tree care company was working in the backyard of a residence. the employeewas working on a tree crew trimming a tree located approximately 9 feet from a 21-kilvolt (kv) power line. coworker #1 was operating a crane from a neighboring yard removing the portions of the tree that the employee had cut away. coworker #2 was standing on the ground approximately 10 feet from the tree. the employeewas elevated off the ground in the tree and had made a cut through the trunk of the tree. coworker #1 was lifting the section from the area when the limbs cameinto contact with the energized lines shocking the employee causing him to lose consciousness. the employee was wearing fall protection at the time. coworker #1 attempted to climb to rescue the employee when he received a shock as well and climbed back down. the employee regained consciousness and self-rescued coming down to the ground where he remained until paramedics arrived. both the employeeand coworker #1 sustained exterior bruising and shock injuries, but neither sustained serious injuries. the employee received medical treatment but was not hospitalized.

at 9:40 a.m. on june 26, 2020, an employee working for a construction equipment rental company was using a multimeter to assess a power fault. the employee was exposed to an arc-flash from contact with a backplane flash. the employee was hospitalized to treat burns to his face and eyes. he was admitted for observation for heart rhythm monitoring to ensure he did not develop cardiac rhythm. there was no irregular heart rhythm at the time he was observed.

at 8:45 a.m. on september 22, 2020, employee #1 was working for a framing contractor. employee #1 and employee #2 were setting 1st roof truss from an endframe wall. a coworker was operating telehandler with a work platform that had several 12' 2x4s used to brace roof trusses.  telehandler extended too far (app. 20') and elevated too high (app. 15-16').  the telehandler became overloaded and tipped forward into the post frame wall that employee #1 and employee #2 were on. the wall fell while employees #1 & #2 were on it. employees #1 & #2 received multiple injuries and were hospitalized.

at 7:24 a.m. on september 6, 2020, four employees were reported hospitalized for covid-19 over a four-month period. the work site is a hospital with 315 employees, 22 covid-19 beds, 1 isolation room and hepa filters are installed in all inpatient rooms. patients are pre-screened in a pop-up tent before being allowed into the facility and those exhibiting covid-19 related symptoms are separated and evaluated. those who may need hospitalization are sent to an isolation room in the er for evaluation. all patients are masked when transported. there is a separate tent for patients suspected of having covid-19 and they are tested onsite. regularly scheduled hospital patients are pre-screened over the telephone and are required to have a negative covid-19 test within 3 days prior to elective procedures. employee #1 is a registered nurse who was hospitalized on september 6, 2020. employee #2 is an er technicianwho was hospitalized on september 15, 2020, employee #3 is a patient access/patient advocate who was hospitalized december 15, 2020, who registers patients and works in a business office away from the main hospital. when the onset occurred employee #3 had been on restricted duty due to an ankle injury and was not required to travel to patient rooms as usual. employee #3 had been fit tested on an n95, which was available when she did have to go to patient rooms. no coworkers were ill with covid-19 at the time, so her illness is presumed to be a community acquired infection. employee #4 is a housekeeper who was hospitalized on january 16, 2021. employee #4 has no patient contact but does clean patient rooms after they have been vacated. employee #4 was fit tested on an n95, provided full ppe and had no contact with the general public other than passing them in the corridor. a number of coworkers were ill approximately 2 weeks prior to onset, but there was no contact with them. employee #4 was presumed to have community acquired infection.

at 3:50 p.m. on october 19, 2020, an employee working for a plastic film manufacturer was cutting grooves in manufactured materials. the employee had removed the carriage support from the mandrel prior to stopping the head/shaft rotation which caused the mandrel to jump. the employee attempted to catch the falling rotating shaft at which point it struck his right arm. the employee shattered his arm, breaking it in two places and requiring hospitalization.
at 1:45 p.m. on september 29, 2020, an employee was installing roofing materials.  the employee fell from an elevation and suffered a concussion.  the employee was hospitalized.

at 11:00 a.m. on october 27, 2020, an employee operated as a licensed contract fabricator and installer of custom, high-end architectural woodworking and millwork for commercial building projects. the employee was a cabinet maker/journeyman and was an employee of wb powell inc. the employee was tested for covid-19 on september 29, 2020 due to other employees in the workplace testing positive. positive test results were received on october 1, 2020, the employee's last day in the workplace. the employee was hospitalized due to complications from covid-19 on october 4, 2020 and died on october 27, 2020.

at 2:40 p.m. on may 26, 2020, an employee working as a construction laborer for a county resource management agency was using a post hole digger to dig a hole for a sign. while performing the strenuous work in the heat, the employee reported that he began to feel ill. the employee received medical treatment for heat exhaustion and dehydration but was not hospitalized.

an employee was working with a crew of nurses providing assistance to a patient. the employee made contact with patient body fluids that were infected with covid-19. the employee contracted covid-19 and subsequently died from her infection at 9:00 a.m. on october 31, 2020.

at 9:30 p.m. on october 28, 2020, an employee working for a concrete products manufacturer was working with a coworker clearing out jammed molds against an air trolley on the back of line 1. as the employee removed one of the jammed molds, unexpected built-up pressure in the air cylinder caused the air trolley to move back to its original position. the trolley struck the employee and pinned his legs against the metal structure of the machine. the employee fractured his right fibula and tibia and was hospitalized requiring surgery.

at 11:20 am on july 27, 2020, employees #1, #2, #3, #4 were inside a new construction commercial building, employees #2, #3, and #4 were working from roof trusses to install plywood sheathing and employee #1 was at an unspecified location in the building. at some point, the building collapsed following a windstorm and all four employees were hospitalized with unspecified injuries where employee #1 later died.

at 9:00 a.m. on june 22, 2020, an employee was in the process of conducting preventive maintenance on the chain drive of a conveyor system. the employee removed the guard on the chain drive to clean and lubricate the chain without turning off the power.  the rag he was using became caught in the chain.  the employee's right hand was caught in between the chain and sprocket.  the employee was taken to loma linda university medical center for an amputation of the right index, middle, and ring fingers but was not hospitalized.

at 8:30 a.m. on november 19, 2020, employee #1, employed by a construction company, was working at a multi-employer job site, a new eight-story commercial building.  he was working from an open-edged, unguarded outrigger platform on the seventh floor and had just finished loading a skip container with debris when the employee fell to the fourth floor, a fall height of 30 feet.  employee #1 sustained blunt force traumatic injuries and was killed.

at 4:45 p.m. on november 25, 2020, employee #1 was washing and rinsing an impeller pump. employee #1 was cleaning honey syrup from the housing and interior spaces of a portable impeller pump.  employee #1 reenergized the impeller pump during the cleaning process.  while reaching to reposition the pump, employee #1's left index finger inadvertently entered the pump outlet. the employee's fingertip contacted the spinning pump impeller. employee #1 sustained a fingertip amputation on the left index finger and was treated without hospitalization.

at 12:49 p.m. on december 7, 2020, employee #1 and a coworker, two day laborers, employed by lake view tree service, were trimming two 45-foot canary island date palms in the front yard of a residence in fallbrook, ca. employee #1 was on the ground feeding palm fronds into a vermeer brush chipper, while another coworker was cutting fronds from the trees. during the process, employee #1 fed a palm frond with a rope still attached into the brush chipper. the rope, which was wrapped around employee #1's leg, violently pulled him toward the chipper, slamming him against the side of the machine before the emergency stop mechanism activated. employee #1 suffered a perineum avulsion requiring a diverting colostomy. it was later discovered that the employees had received no safety training, and no supervisor was present at the site at the time of the accident. the employee was hospitalized.

at approximately 10:00 p.m. on december 8, 2020, an employee was conducting a first-run drying process on a chlorinated solid material in a double cone dryer. at approximately 10:00 pm, the employee left the control room to start adding cooling water to the jacket of the double cone dryer. the employee was standing on the second-story catwalk above the double cone dryer when the double cone dryer exploded.  the employee was transported to the hospital.  the employee received fatal injuries and died from the trauma from the explosion.

at approximately 7:30 p.m. on december 14, 2020, an employee was hospitalized since november 27, 2020.  on november 19, 2020, the employee called off work illand contacted their doctor for a covid-19 test. on november 20, 2020, the employee notified their employer that they had tested positive for covid-19. the employee continued to stay home until november 27, 2020. on november 27, 2020, the employee was admitted to the hospital. on december 14, 2020, the employee died from complications from the virus.

at 11:00 a.m. on november 24, 2020, an employee who worked as a social worker at a military installation contracted covid-19. on that same date, the employee had attended a meeting where he reportedly came into close contact with a coworker with suspected of being covid-19 positive. on november 26, 2020, the employee began exhibiting symptoms of covid-19 and was admitted to the hospital. on december 1, 2020, the employee tested positive for covid-19. on december 8, 2020, the employee was released from the hospital. on december 9, 2020, the employee was found unresponsive at home and had died.

at 7:05 a.m. on december 15, 2020, employee #1 was moving a pallet containing rolls of paper. while pulling the pallet backwards, the employee walked into the travel lane of a forklift operator, who subsequently struck the employee. as a result, employee #1 sustained a fractured right ankle and was hospitalized for treatment.

at 3:15 p.m. on december 17, 2020, an employee was adjusting the depth of the knife blade when his left hand and arm became caught in the rotating product as it wound around a spool. the incident resulted in the complete amputation of the employee's left arm. the lack of adequate machine guarding contributed to the severity of the injury.

at approximately 9:30 a.m. on december 22, 2020, an employee was working on grinder line #4, which is an industrial-sized conveyor belt.  the employee was working to toss plastic lids onto the top of the conveyor belt. the conveyor belt was configured and set up to be inclined at a 45-degree angle.  the employee's job was to toss plastic lids via the top of the conveyor. the conveyor moved the plastic lids upwards and into a grinder. the conveyor belt speed moved at approximately 3 to 5 miles per hour.  the employee saw that somedebris was caught underneath the grinder line #4 conveyor belt.  the employee reached underneath this conveyor belt.  the employee was able to get immediate and direct access to the underside of the grinder line #4 conveyor belt since it was unguarded.  as the employee reached in, the employee's right arm was caughtbetween the nip point and the belt. following this, the employee's entire body was pulled in. in the immediate vicinity, was a large protruding bolt, approximately 9 inches long with a 1 inch diameter thread width and a 2 inch diameter head width. this protruding bolt was a part of the engineered design of grinder line #4. as the employee's body was being pulled in, the employee's neck was pulled into and through this bolt. the employee was killed.

at 1:00 p.m. on january 15, 2021, an employee entered a straight walled, thirty-foot long, five to seven-foot high and four to six-foot wide trench to assist three coworkers to install a six-inch pvc sanitary sewer line. shortly after entering the trench, a section of the northwest wall collapsed and buried the employee. the employee was killed by traumatic asphyxia.

at 12:30 a.m. on january 20, 2021, an employee was preparing an aircraft tire to be removed from the split-rim system by utilizing a pneumatic air gun, pliers/grips, a pry bar, and a toyota powered industrial truck (pit). the pit was used to hold the tire in an upright  position between the forks. the split-rim system is held together by bolts that go through he inner side to the outer side where lug nuts are located. when installing  tires  onto the rims the lug nuts are tightened and then secured by cottar pins. the tire pressure had not been relieved prior to the employee removing the cottar pins on the lug nuts.  when a sufficient number of cottar pins were removed there was a catastrophic seal failure and a resultant release of intense pressure, which resulted in both halves of the tire rim to become projectiles. the employee was struck by one half of the split-rim and was thrown approximately 23 feet into adjacent metal tires racks, suffering blunt force injuries to the torso, extremities and head, killing him. the employee struck the racks with enough force to break the welds on the racking system, and subsequently hit the ground. the inner projectile rim traveled an additional 40 feet coming to rest approximately 6 feet outside of the shops rolling doors. the other half of the rim went through two industrial workshop tables coming to rest below the second table.
at 12:00 p.m. on december 23, 2020, an employee was working as a housekeeper and cook at a group home for the developmentally disabled persons. the employee became infected with covid-19 and died.

at 9:00 p.m. on october 17, 2020, an employee working as a laborer for a building services company was working with a crew that was using a crane to move large 1500-pound bags of product from a pit measuring approximately 26-foot by 20-foot wide by 15-foot to forklifts. the employee placed a 16-foot ladder inside the pit to allow access and egress from the pit. he placed the ladder topside down so that the dogs did not rip or damage the bags. as the crew removed the bags, the employee made adjustments to the ladder until the pit was empty. the employee attempted to climb out of the pit with the ladder still inserted the wrong way causing the ladder to slide out from under him. he landed on the ground and had to be extricated by local fire department using the crane. the ladder was still in place with the top of the ladder facing away from the front of the pit with the feet/dogs against the wall closer to the front. the employee was hospitalized to treat leg injuries.

on saturday, december 12 and sunday december 13, 2020, employee # 1 was working in a brigade when a coworker tested positive for covid-19. employee # 1 went into quarantine according to protocol. on december 17, 2020, employee # 1 tested positive for covid-19. he was hospitalized until december 30, 2020, when he died of medical complications. he died at 3:51 p.m. of heart attack and respiratory complications from covid-19.

at 1:07 a.m. on september 21, 2020, employee #1 was working at a car wash and oil change service bay, standing in front of a vehicle he was servicing. at the same time, a coworker was driving a truck that come out of the carwash bay. as the coworker proceeded to drive the truck a few yards away, he lost control of the vehicle and crashed into the oil change service bay exit doors. employee #1 was struck by the out-of-control vehicle and suffered a laceration to his left leg. employee #1 was hospitalized and received surgical repair of the muscle tissue.

at  7:15 p.m. on january 29, 2021, an employee was cleaning shot off a flat plate that runs on rollers leading to a wheelabrator. the employee's gloved left hand was caught between the plate and the rollers in a pinch point.  he received crushing injuries to the four fingers of the left hand, resulting in amputations of the middle and ring fingertips and was hospitalized.

at 3:30 p.m. on august 18, 2020, employee #1 was working for commercial general contractor.  employee #1 was shoveling hot pavement when he began to feel symptoms of heat illness. the outside temperature was 110 degrees f, and employee #1 was working in a hot process.  employee #1 received medical treatment and was hospitalized.

at 10:00 p.m. on january 26, 2021, an employee was servicing the water pump of a tanker truck. the employee's clothing was caught in the water pump drive shaft and the employee was killed when he was decapitated.

at 11:00 a.m. on february 19, 2021, two employees were cleaning out a barge. employee #1 was on top of the barge when he fell, landing on employee #2 who was inside the barge hopper. employee #1 sustained a closed head injury, and employee #2 suffered a shoulder injury along with a fractured clavicle. employee #1 was treated without hospitalization, and employee #2 was hospitalized to treat the fracture.

at 9:30 p.m. on february 1, 2021, employee #1 was performing sanitization and cleaning of a pneumatically operated technal cheese curd press. the employee did not deenergize the pneumatic system or bleed residual pressurized air before cleaning. while cleaning a section of the machine, the pneumatically operated lever activation switch advanced forward, pressing the right side of the employee's face against the machine frame. the impact caused a minor fracture on the left side of the employee's jaw. the employee was transported to highland hospital, where she was admitted and discharged on february 2, 2021.

at 4:45 p.m. on october 21, 2020, an employee working as a supervisor for a grocery products wholesaler was attempting to get the avatar form and fill machine operating. while she was positioned on the left side of the equipment with it running, the employee made contact with the jaws and blade. the employeereceived medical treatment without hospitalization for an amputated the left index finger.

at 2:00 p.m. on december 14, 2020, an employee who worked as a endoscopy technician at a community hospital was hospitalized with covid-19. coworker #1, who was a registered nurse, and coworker #2, who was an executive secretary, were also hospitalized. on or before december 14, 2020, the employee tested positive for covid-19 prior to or during his hospitalization. on january 20, 2021, coworker #1 was admitted to the hospital due to complications from covid-19 related pneumonia. on february 6, 2021, coworker #22 was admitted to the hospital due to complications related to covid-19. despite the three cases of hospitalization due to covid-19, the exposure could not be traced to the workplace.

an employee was conducting home health aid care to an elderly man. the employee's last day of work was february 5, 2021. the employee began having symptoms of covid-19 on february 11, 2021, was hospitalized on february 15, 2021, and died march 3, 2021.

at approximately 12:00 p.m. on march 1, 2021, an employee, who was a road service mechanic, became ill and died from covid-19. on march 2, 2021, the division was contacted by the employer who reported the death of an employee dueto covid-19.

at 3:30 p.m. on march 13, 2021, an employee was walking in a closed roadway lane next to a sod trailer. a vehicle swerved off the roadway and struck the employee. the employee suffered multiple blunt force traumas and was killed.

at 1:15 p.m. on march 11, 2021, an employee was removing material caught at the conveyor roller drum.  the employee's right arm was trapped by the conveyor roller drum.  the employee's right arm was amputated below the shoulder, and the employee was hospitalized.
an employee was working as a maintenance mechanic in a correctional facility when he contracted covid-19 and required hospitalization for treatment of his infection on 12:15 p.m. november 19, 2020.

an employee reported working on monday, february 22, 2021.  the employee went through the plant's covid-19 screening process upon arriving at work.  the employee did not register a fever and reported no symptoms during screening. the employee worked from 7 a.m. to 11 a.m., doing his normal job as a shipping supervisor, which included overseeing forklift operators on the shipping dock. at 11 a.m., the employee said did not feel right, so he left work.  on tuesday, february 23, 2021, the employee went to see the doctor, who did a covid-19 test, and the test result came back positive.  the doctor sent the employee to the emergency department and was admitted to the hospital.  while hospitalized, the employee had another covid-19 test, which also came back positive.  the hospital discharged the employee on february 26, 2021.    on tuesday, march 2, 2021, the employee went to the emergency department due to worsening symptoms.  the emergency department treated and released the employee.  on thursday, march 4, 2021, the employee went to a follow-up appointment with his family physician, who sent the employee back to the hospital.  the employee remained hospitalized, and on march 18, 2021, at 9:30 a.m., the employee died from covid-19 cardiac arrest while still hospitalized.

at 8:45 a.m. on december 2, 2020, three employees (1 registered nurse, 1 housekeeper and a laundry aid) who worked for a skilled nursing facility were reportedly hospitalized with covid-19. all three employees had been performing their normally assigned duties prior to contracting covid-19. all three employees were hospitalized and released. the date, time and other details leading up to the exposures are not indicated in the narrative. the employer did not have adequate aerosol transmissible diseases policies and procedures in place.

at 8:00 a.m. on march 22, 2021, employee #1 was observing a boring operation conducted by a horizontal drill rig. employee #1 was struck by a 1.25-inch diameter carbon steel pipe as it spun out of control. employee received blunt force to the chest and was killed by suffocation.

at approximately 11:10 p.m. on december 30, 2024, employee #1, a 33-year-old male employed full-time by great northern corporation, was cleaning the feed table of an emba multi-color flexo folder gluer machine (model: 170, machine no. 771066). the employee, assisted by a helper, was hand-feeding large cardboard stock (approximately 17" x 12") into the machine. after noticing the cardboard feeding incorrectly, employee #1 stopped the machine and went to the feed table to inspect. suspecting dust buildup, he applied a silicone spray and used a folded rag to clean the area. while wiping near the machine's in running nip point, the rag was drawn into the rollers through a 0.375" gap, pulling in the employee's hand. the helper was unable to see the incident but quickly halted operations. emergency services, including the fire department, arrived and used the jaws-of-life to release the employee's hand. he was transported to the hospital, where he received treatment for a degloving injury and finger amputations without hospitalization.

an employee was on vacation for approximately one week and returned to normal duties in the meat department for  one day.  the employee contracted covid-19 and died from covid-19 complications on march 28, 2021.

at 10:30 a.m. on april 2, 2021, an employee was operating a forklift to move a nearly full tote containing vanwet 9n9 surfactant, which weighed approximately 2,420 pounds when full. the tote was raised about 4 feet off the ground in a heat room to transfer product into five-gallon buckets. employees typically stood in front of the elevated tote to operate a valve at its base. at the time, no other employees were in the heat room. employees working nearby noticed the forklift had been in the heat room longer than usual and went to investigate. when they arrived, they found the employee pinned between the ground and industrial storage racks. the tote had slid off the forklift and was resting on one side of the forks. despite efforts from nearby employees to move the tote using a forklift, it remained on top of the employee. a group of about seven employees eventually freed the individual, but the employee died from asphyxiation due to the weight of the tote, which was approximately 80-90% fullat the time of the incident.

at 1:30 p.m. on april 6, 2021, an employee was operating a standing electric forklift in an aisle, placing a pallet on the bottom floor under a rack. during the operation, the employee's arm became pinned between the forklift and a racking pillar. the employee sustained two fractures to the arm and was hospitalized for treatment.

at 10:00 a.m. on april 5, 2021, employee #1, employed by a company operating overhead bridge cranes, was positioning a track welder on the side of an industrial silencer. during the process, the lifting hook from the hoist of the bridge crane grabbed onto his left hand and pushed him off the scissor lift. the employee fell approximately 12 feet to a hard paved surface. employee #1 sustained unspecified injuries.  emergency services transported the employee to the hospital, where he was admitted and is recovering from the incident.

at 2:15 a.m. on february 18, 2020, the employee was sanitizing the dough wrapper machine. the employee turned on the dough wrapper machine to sanitize the roller. the employee was alone in the dough-wrapping department, when she turned the machine on. as the employee turned on the machine, she proceeded to wipe the rollers with a rag, as the rollers spun. the employee sustained unspecified injuries and was treated without hospitalization.  the machine should have beenoff when she was sanitizing the dough wrapper. additionally, the employee confirmed through the interview, the site employer or the staffing company never properly trained her. as a result, citations were issued to the employer.
at 6:45 p.m. on april 22, 2021, an employee was operating an aerial bucket lift trimming a tree when the employee contacted energized power lines. the employeewas electrocuted and was killed.

an employee was a janitor providing cleaning services in the office area of a building. half way through his shift, human resources approached him concerning that he was possibly exhibiting of symptoms of covid and sent him home to get tested. two days later the employee was checked into a hospital for covid-19. the employer had one confirmed case of covid-19, and the employee was out on quarantine. the employee worked an off-shift with no interaction with other employees from 4 a.m. - 8 a.m. and had very minimal contact with staff from 8 a.m. to 12 p.m.  the employee did not work with or have any contact with the quarantined employee. the employer had all covid-19 protocols in place per the emergency temporary standard and was  conducting its own contact tracing.  the employee died of covid-19 at 11:30 a.m. on may 10, 2021.

at approximately 9:39 a.m. on may 11, 2021, an employee was unstrapping a load of granite slabs when the slabs shifted and slid onto the employee, pinning him to the ground. the weight of the granite slabs caused the employee to sustain a broken jaw and deep lacerations to various parts of the body. the employee was hospitalized for treatment.  a second employee sustained minor injuries but was not hospitalized.  the incident involved shifting granite slabs that were inadequately secured during the unstrapping process.

at 2:20 a.m. on april 16, 2021, employee #1 was waiting for a conveying assembly line to shut down when he saw a piece of cardboard stuck near a drive roller. the employee reached to remove the cardboard, and his left middle fingertip was amputated. the incident involved a belt and pulley on the conveyor. the employeewas treated without hospitalization.

at 1:00 p.m. on november 30, 2020, an employee working as a teacher for an elementary school had been working on her bulletin board. she was standing on top of the cubbies when she jumped from the cubbies to the counter near the sink and fell. the employee struck her head on the concrete floor and sustained a skull fracture. the employee was hospitalized and during an mri, it was discovered she had a brain tumor.

at 10:45 a.m. on april 29, 2021, an employee working as a meat cutter for a wholesale grocery was cutting boneless pork chops. the employee was not utilizing the push plate supplied to feed the meat product into the band saw. as he used his right hand to feed the piece of meat into the saw his hand slipped and contacted the sawblade. the employee was hospitalized to treat a lacerated index finger.

an employee was working as an administrative assistant for a housing development when she became infected with covid-19. the employee's symptoms worsened and she subsequently died due to covid-19 complications on april 9, 2021.

at approximately 3:00 p.m. on june 10, 2021, an employee was constructing a new electrical distribution line.  the employee was connecting a neutral line near energized primary phases and contacted the energized phase. the employee contacted energized primary phase with his face and was holding a grounded neutral phase.  the employee was hospitalized for electrical burns to the face and arm.

an employee developed symptoms on december 11, 2020, and was tested on december 14, 2020.  the employee received a positive result on december 16, 2020, and was hospitalized on december 21, 2020.  the employee was later released from the hospital.

at approximately 1:30 p.m. on june 22, 2021, employee #1 and a coworker were using a 14-inch chop saw to cut long threaded bars into 6-inch pieces. during the cutting process, one of the long bars dropped. as employee #1 reached to pick it up, his right hand made contact with the unguarded rotating saw blade. he sustained a partial amputation of the right thumb. the employee was treated for his injuries without hospitalization.

at 12:30 p.m. on june 30, 2021, an employee arrived at the job site and continued to sit in their vehicle. approximately two hours later coworkers went to the vehicle to check on the employee and found the employee unresponsive. the employee was killed by cardiovascular disease.

at 8:30 a.m. on june 30, 2021, an employee working for a meat supplier was cleaning out a mixer. while the employee was cleaning, a coworker turned on the machine in order to use the mixer. the employee had his left hand near the blade as it started to rotate. the employee was hospitalized to treat an amputated index fingertip.

at 6:40 a.m. on july 7, 2021, an employee was struck by a box truck that began to back up to leave for another worksite. the truck rolled over and dragged the employee approximately twenty feet. the truck driver realized a problem, discovered the employee under the truck and used a forklift to raise the truck off the employee. emergency medical services were called, and the employee was transported to the hospital, but died on arrival.
at approximately 6:00 a.m. on september 12, 2014, an employee was directing the medical-surgical 3c unit. the employee had a migraine headache and fever. the employee was hospitalized for covid-19.

at 7:00 a.m. on july 13, 2021, an employee was performing electrical 208 and 120-volt ac service and troubleshooting on a steel stretcher leveler machine. while working on live electrical components in a 480-volt power cabinet, an arcflash occurred, resulting in the employee experiencing an electric shock. the employee was hospitalized.

at 2:45 p.m. on july 20, 2021, employee #1 was disconnecting a hose on top of a tanker and closing the tanker using a cage with an attached gangway stair lowered from an overhead catwalk. while she was on top of the truck, the truck driver began moving the truck backward. with the gangway stair and safety cage still in place, her legs became caught between the cage and the trailer. employee #1 sustained two broken bones in her right leg, a fractured left leg, and a small fracture to her spine. she was hospitalized for treatment.

at 11:22 a.m. on february 15, 2020, employee #1, employed by an industrial building construction contractor, was climbing on a ceiling panel in a warehouse 13 feet above the warehouse floor. employee #1 fell to the concrete floor of the warehouse because the ceiling panel was not secured.  employee #1 suffered broken ribs, a punctured spleen, hearing loss, and gangrene on the toes. employee # 1 was hospitalized.

at 11:30 p.m. on july 26, 2021, an employee was clearing paper plates from a dyepress machine when they opened a gate/guard to remove debris by hand. the machine did not stop when the gate was opened, and the employee's left hand was caught, resulting in the amputation of four fingers. the employee was hospitalized for treatment.

at 2:40 p.m. on february 10, 2021, employee #1 was cutting stock material in the advanced product development department of an aeronautical manufacturing facility using a drake/johnson horizontal band saw (model #j10 & jh10). while the saw was in operation, the employee's hand became caught in the unguarded blade, resulting in the amputation of a finger. the saw lacked a proper guard, which contributed to the incident. employee #1 was treated at loma linda hospital and released without hospitalization.

at 7:30 p.m. on july 2, 2021, employee # 1, employee #2, and employee #3, were performing two maintenance tasks in the m2 crude rectification section on the ground and second levels at tower m2-t250 and vessel v-207. on july 2, 2021, during the night shift, three (3) gas monomer chemical operators were exposed to toxic fluorocarbons and other hazardous chemicals while performing the maintenance tasks at m2-t250 that resulted in delayed respiratory failure.  the three employees were exposed to unknown and known acute toxic chemicals identified as expected to be in the process streams such as, but not limited to, perfluorocyclobutane (c318), perfluoroisobutene (pfib), octafluoroisomethylether (oime), and/or hydrogen fluoride (hf) when returning t250 back to normal operations due to inadequate safe work practices and respiratory protection. on july 4 after employee #1 and employee #2 's shift ended, they had problems breathing that progressively worsened and they went separately to the hospital where they were admitted for respiratory failure. employee #2 later died from respiratory failure. then, employee #3  worked his shift on july 3, and on july 4, the employee had trouble breathing that was worsening when he walked to his car.  the employee was admitted for respiratory failure.  the employee later died from respiratory failure.

at 2:20 p.m. on july 1, 2021, employee #1 had finished mowing lawns at a job site and packed up their gear before getting into the company truck to return to the yard. while riding in the vehicle, the employee felt nauseous and vomited. the employee was taken to a clinic and subsequently transported to henry mayo hospital. the employee was diagnosed with anemia, with heat exposure possibly contributing to the condition. the employee was hospitalized for treatment.

at 9:50 p.m. on august 25, 2021, an employee was attempting to access a roof where a coworker was pressure washing the hood vent for a kitchen grill on a restaurant.  the coworker heard a voice and saw the employee rising up from the ladder over the edge of the roof however, it was dark and the coworker lost sight of the employee. the coworker exited the roof and returned to the ground where he found the employee injured. the employee had fallen approximately 20-feet when he attempted to get onto the roof from the ladder. the employee was killed and suffered fatal blunt force trauma to the body.

on march 8, 2021, an employee was parked overnight waiting for deliveries. while waiting, he was intermittently using a portable generator. during a welfare check,  he was found dead in his vehicle of possible carbon monoxide poisoning.

at 10:00 a.m. on march 15, 2021, an employee was responding to a repair request for the honor vl-125c cnc machine, a vertical turning lathe.   the employee was working alone.  the machine was in manual data input (mdi) mode, and the employee input a m03 "spindle forward set" command instead of the m06 "auto toolchange" command into the control panel of the machine.  the key to the interlock had been left in the machine from a previous maintenance of the machine, and it had been turned to the "o" position.  the "o" position disabled the interlock function of the door, which normally would cause the machine to turn off.  the employee was caught in the rotating table.  the employee suffered a torn ligament and lacerations to his left knee and was hospitalized.

at 8:30 a.m. on august 26, 2021, two employees who both had been providing homehealthcare to covid-19 positive residents were reported hospitalized due to complications from contracting covid-19. the date, time and other details leading up to exposure are not indicated in the narrative. information regarding engineering, administrative and personal protective equipment controls for covid-19 are also not included in the narrative.

at 3:00 p.m. on may 8, 2021, an employee working for a delivery service was unhooking a trailer from a tractor. the employee was moving to unhook the dolly from lead trailer on the drivers blind side when the driver pulled forward. the employee was struck by the dolly and hospitalized to treat a facial fracture.

an employee was working as a laborer for a poultry processor. due to household contact with someone who was positive (or thought to be positive) for covid-19, he had been quarantined from august 25, 2021, until september 3, 2021. he had returned to work for one day. on september 3, 2021, his last day worked, he began exhibiting symptoms for covid-19 at work. he was sent home to be tested. on september 4, he was confirmed positive for covid-19. he was admitted to a hospital on september 8, 2021. he died there at 12:05 a.m. on september 23, 2021.

an employee was doing normal housekeeping duties.  the employee developed covid-19 symptoms and tested positive for covid-19. the employee died from complications from covid-19 at approximately 2:45 p.m. on september 17, 2021.

at 3:48 p.m. on july 23, 2021, an employee was standing near a core-making machine when the cope section stuck to the tamping plate instead of lowering as intended. as the box shuttled out, the cope came loose, fell from the tamping plate, and struck the employee. the employee sustained a broken leg and was hospitalized. the incident occurred indoors under adequate lighting.

at 8:57 a.m. on october 1, 2021, an employee working as a heavy equipment operator for a waste management company was inflating a tire from a piece of heavy equipment. the tire exploded and struck the employee. the employee broke his left arm and fractured 6 ribs, requiring hospitalization.

at 11:14 p.m. on may 20, 2021, an employee working for a children's hospital was performing a one-one-one with a patient. the patient became agitated and struck the employee with their fist after their behavior escalated. the employee was hospitalized to treat head trauma including a concussion, bruising and contusions.

at 9:30 a.m. on october 13, 2021, an employee, a cdl truck driver, was trying to fix the air brake system on a fellow truck driver's peanut trailer. the employeewas run over by the peanut trailer. the employee sustained severe head and torsotrauma as a result of being run over by the trailer.   the employee was killed.

at 8:15 a.m. on may 26, 2021, an employee was feeding a pipe through a machine and checking it for scratches when his hand was pulled into the sizing rolls. the employee sustained an unspecified amputation. the employee was treated without hospitalization.

at 7:00 a.m. on october 19, 2021, an employee was working on the cone line at a poultry processing plant.  the employee was retrieving cut chicken strips from the conveyor system's plastic chain belting by hand.  her finger wedged betweena chain link and an undetermined fixed component of the conveyor, resulting in an amputation of the tip of her index finger.  the employee was treated withouthospitalization.

at 3:00 p.m. on october 22, 2021, an employee worked to install an overhead support beam in a kitchen nook area. the employee used a fourteen-foot long two by six-inch piece of lumber, nailed on one side, as a makeshift scaffold platform to access the area. the employee stopped while crossing the platform to use a pneumatic nail gun to install framing near the center of the board. as the employee applied pressure to nail the framing overhead, the board broke and the employee fell. the employee was transported by emergency medical services to the hospital, where the employee died from the injuries.

at 8:12 a.m. on october 30, 2021, an employee was working for a company that produces charcoal. the employee was getting a water hose to extinguish smoldering wood and was crossing a concrete pad used for cooling the charcoal when she was struck by a caterpillar, model 924h, front-end loader. the employeesuffered multiple traumatic injuries and died as a result.

at 1:13 p.m. on november 29, 2021, employee #1, the lead field service technician, was repairing a vinyl curtain in the gym of a high school. employee #1 was raised in an elevated platform (i.e., bucket/basket) of a genie model number awp-25s lift that was without power, while a coworker, a field technician, was on the ground pushing the lift. the lift rolled over and

at approximately 5:55 a.m. on november 24,  2021, an employee was processing parts on the press brake of a cycle press.  the employee's right index finger was caught in between the dies. the employee sustained amputation right index fingertip. the employee was hospitalized for these injuries.
